danger_signals.visual_form: Adjacent provinces on tactics screen map display different owner colors
danger_signals.meaning: Threat of attack; vulnerable border requires defense or preemptive action

danger_signals.visual_form: Resource panel on left side of tactics screen shows depleted counts
danger_signals.meaning: Territory weakness; inability to hire/upgrade units or sustain military operations

danger_signals.visual_form: Turn-based battle screen shows unit removal or health depletion
danger_signals.meaning: Loss of tactical advantage; risk of territory capture if main base is reached

danger_signals.visual_form: Battle outcome condition when enemy army runs out of food
danger_signals.meaning: Imminent enemy surrender or army dissolution

safety_signals.visual_form: Provinces displayed in player's color on world map
safety_signals.meaning: Secure zone under player command; safe from external attack

safety_signals.visual_form: Diplomatic action completion on tactics screen
safety_signals.meaning: Non-aggression pact; reduced threat from allied faction

safety_signals.visual_form: Defense upgrade action completed on tactics screen
safety_signals.meaning: Increased castle resistance to enemy siege; improved garrison strength

safety_signals.visual_form: Battle screen shows all five fielded units active and healthy
safety_signals.meaning: Peak combat readiness; tactical advantage in current engagement

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Led by Prince Erin; supported by Zendor (Emerald Wizard wielding Lightning magic)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One of two most powerful clans; controls gem-affiliated territory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Led by Prince Ander; backed by Pluvius (Sapphire Wizard casting Meteors)
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: One of two most powerful clans; controls gem-affiliated territory

faction_indicators.enemy_visual_variants: Wizard: Scylla; holder of one wizard gem
Wizard: Empyron; holder of one wizard gem
Wizard: Chylla; holder of one wizard gem
Wizard: Skulryk; holder of one wizard gem
Tyrannical incumbent; currently controls Gemfire crown artifact
faction_indicators.environmental_markers: Princess Robyn imprisoned in tower; represents corrupted authority

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Six wizard gems scattered across island territories, each claimed by competing family
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: All families engage in territorial conquest; multiple simultaneous military campaigns

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Crown artifact held as central power symbol; royal tower contains imprisoned princess
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Subjects oppressed; families motivated to unseat incumbent and seize throne

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Power balance shift - gem collection by player
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Captured wizard gems visible on tactics screen as player resources; territory color shifts to player ownership
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Enemy AI responds with increased aggression toward player; alliances shift based on player threat level

world_state_to_visual.world_state: Military dominance - player controls majority territories
world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Map display shows player color occupying large contiguous regions
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Remaining factions may seek alliance or abandon weaker territories; unification victory condition approaches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: Screen shifts from tactics overview to tactical battle map with individual unit placement
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: Units move in turn-based sequence; terrain features (fences) become relevant for defensive positioning

environmental_storytelling.example: Isle of Ishmeria divided into provinces; each holds gem-affiliated family, telling story of dispersed power after Gemfire's destruction
Gemfire crown was symbol of unified authority; now broken into six gems, visually representing political fragmentation across families
Princess Robyn locked in tower after breaking crown; creates emotional motivation for all factions to claim throne and restore order
Each family's association with specific wizard (lightning, meteors, etc.) creates visual/mechanical distinction beyond territorial color
Four scenarios use same game board but alter family positions, provinces, vassals, and gem distribution—tells different political narratives from same map

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: Casts elemental magic (lightning, meteors) according to type; provides significant tactical advantage
Increases military production; may initiate attack on adjacent player territories; seeks alliances with other families
May propose alliance or plunder actions to gain resources; defensive posture on remaining provinces
Automatic defeat condition triggered; territory remains vulnerable to capture
Castle shows increased visual fortification; inflicts higher casualties on attacking forces
Functions as alternative to wizard; fills fifth unit slot; provides tactical flexibility without committing faction wizard

notes: Gemfire communicates world-state primarily through territory map coloring (faction ownership), resource panel displays (safety/vulnerability), and diplomatic/military action outcomes on tactics screen. The game lacks detailed visual effects or dynamic environmental changes; communication is abstract and menu-driven. Faction identity relies on narrative (wizard affiliation) rather than visual distinction beyond territorial color. The dual-screen layout (tactics overview + battle detail) creates meta-narrative of strategic planning versus tactical execution. Limited source material restricts specificity on exact visual representations.
